content.full_text:
.
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
July 31, 1986

Mr. Michael T. LeSage
LaSage Senate Campaign
620 13th Street
Post Office Box 306
Paso Robles, CA 93447

RE: FPPC Advice No. A-86-228

Dear Mr. LeSage:

Thank you for your letter of July 11, 1986, concerning the
legality of holding raffles.

FACTS

The LeSage Senate Campaign proposes to issue free raffle
tickets to the general public, regardless of party affiliation.
The ticket will qualify the holder to participate in a raffle
for a color television. In addition, a bonus prize will be
given if the holder of the winning raffle ticket possesses a
ballot stub verifying he or she voted in the November, 1986
election.

QUESTION

You have asked if there are any legal restrictions
prohibiting this type of activity.

ANSWER

The Commission cannot advise you as to the legality of
holding a raffle. You need to contact the State Attorney
General's office at 800/952-5225 for an interpretation of Penal
Code Section 319 which prohibits some types of raffles and
regarding Elections Code Sections 29621-29622 which prohibit
inducements to vote. In addition, Elections Code Section 12400,
et. seq designates permissible uses of campaign funds. You
should also ask the State Attorney General's office if the money
spent on the television and bonus prize intended for the raffle
is an acceptable use of campaign funds.

Please do not hesitate to contact me if I may be of further
assistance to you in the future.

Sincerely,

Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

MAK:cah

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

LeSAGE SENATE CAMPAIGN
620 - 13TH STREET
P.O. BOX 306
PASO ROBLES, CALIFORNIA 93447
(805) 238-3484

July 11, 1986


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Office of the Attorney General
555 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Sirs:

As part of our campaign, we propose to issue free raffle
tickets to people we meet. The raffle tickets will qualify
the holder to receive a color television set, and if the
holder of that ticket has voted in the November 3, 1986
election, will qualify the holder of the ticket to receive
an additional bonus. The drawing will occur on November 3,
1986. We intend to distribute these tickets with absolutely
no charge or bias to party. We are trying to encourage
people to vote and are trying to find a means to spark an
interest in the election process.

Please advise us if this practice is within legal bounds or
if there are any indications or cautions that should apply
to it.

Sincerely,

MICHAEL T. LeSAGE

MTL/kz

July 16, 1986


Michael T. LeSage
LeSage Senate Campaign
P.O. Box 306
Paso Robles, CA 93447

Re: 86-228

Dear Mr. LeSage:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act has been received on July 15, 1986 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or unless more information is needed to answer your request,
you should expect a response within 21 working days.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division
